master_row.title: Theorem 5: Quantum Flux Quantization: Φ_oplus ∼ 4× 10^22 Φ_0.
master_row.canonical_statement: Magnetic flux satisfies \[ \Phi = n\Phi_{0}, \qquad \Phi_{0} = \frac{h}{2e}. \] Earth's magnetic flux through the equatorial plane is of order \[ \Phi_{\oplus} \sim 4\times 10^{22}\,\Phi_{0}. \] This topological invariant explains stability over geological timescales—the pattern cannot unwind without violating quantum constraints. The same mechanism stabilizes atomic orbitals and Cooper pairs in superconductors.
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Theorem 5: Quantum Flux Quantization’.
